_meta.source: Department of Basic Education — NSC Examinations; Stats SA Census 2022
_meta.update_frequency: Annual (matric results: January; literacy: decennial census)
_meta.notes: Matric pass rates are published in January each year. Literacy rates come from Census (10-year cycle). Update matric results by running: python scripts/update_education.py

statistics.title: National Matric Pass Rate
statistics.description: The national senior certificate (matric) pass rate for the 2024 academic year, the highest on record. Bachelor's degree passes (qualifying for university entrance) were achieved by 47.8% of candidates. 1,044,415 candidates wrote the examinations.

statistics.title: Adult Literacy Rate (20+)
statistics.description: Proportion of adults aged 20 and older who can read and write, as recorded in the 2022 National Census. This represents significant improvement from 85.0% in 2011. The next census is expected in 2032.

statistics.title: Higher Education Enrolment
statistics.description: Total headcount enrolments at public universities for the 2023 academic year, across 26 public higher education institutions. This excludes TVET colleges and private higher education institutions.

source.name: Department of Higher Education and Training
source.publicationName: Statistics on Post-School Education and Training 2023
